summary: Merged a much more complete fighter.h ahead of fighter.c so downstream fighter work could stay synchronized with master. The PR expanded Fighter, ftCommonData, CollData, ftData-adjacent declarations, fighter/character enums, callback fields, attr layout, fighter.s prototypes, global variables, and assorted function prototypes, then updated existing fighter/player callsites to use the new field names and nesting. The author explicitly noted that some Fighter::attr members and callbacks were not yet used, but were included with comments because they would be needed soon and were based on datamining.

key_files.role: Primary change: expanded Fighter-related declarations, enums, struct fields, attr substruct, callback function pointers, and fighter.s prototypes.
Converted many placeholder declarations into typed or partially typed prototypes used by fighter work.
Declared lbl_804D64FC..lbl_804D6550 as void* globals and retained lbl_804D6540/lbl_804D6554 declarations.
Added inline fpclassify support and FloatType enum.
Updated callsites from raw fighter fields to named fields such as x4_fighterKind and xEC_groundVel.
Updated callback field accesses to named callback members.
Updated callback assignments to named callback members.
Updated player ID references to xC_playerID.
Adjusted attr access from top-level x258 to nested x110_attr.x258.
Removed local extern for func_80016C64 after central prototype was added.
Renamed Player_800326CC vector parameter to out_vec.

what_changed: Added FighterKind enum with FTKIND_* entries for the fighter slot/order, including Mario, Fox, Popo/Nana, MasterHand, CrazyHand, Boy, Girl, GigaBowser, and Sandbag.
Added CharacterKind enum with CKIND_* entries, separate from FighterKind ordering.
Expanded ftCommonData with named fields such as x98_shieldHealthInit, x204_knockbackFrameDecay, x3E8_shieldKnockbackFrameDecay, x3EC_shieldGroundFrictionMultiplier, and x498_ledgeCooldownTime.
Expanded CollData with typed and named fields including x134_envFlags, x14C_groundIndex, and x154_groundNormal.
Added ftHit with filler to offset 0x134.

Replaced large portions of Fighter filler with offset-annotated fields for identity, facing, scale, velocities, positions, movement state, ftData, nested attr data, bones, collision data, hit/damage/shield/grab state, ledge/walljump fields, flags, callbacks, ftcmd vars, and state vars.

Nested fighter attributes under x110_attr and named many datamined attributes such as WalkInitialVelocity, GroundFriction, Gravity, TerminalVelocity, Weight, ModelScaling, landing lag fields, WallJumpHorizontalVelocity, WallJumpVerticalVelocity, and RespawnPlatformScale.

Added callback function pointer names at x2190..x21E8, including OnGrabFighter_Self, IASA, Anim, Phys, Coll, Accessory callbacks, OnGiveDamage, OnShieldHit, OnReflectHit, EveryHitlag, EnterHitlag, ExitHitlag, OnTakeDamage, and OnDeath variants.
Added a block of fighter.s function prototypes in fighter.h, including func_800679B0 through func_8006DABC in the excerpt.

Converted many include/functions.h placeholder comments into declarations, often with partial types or source comments, such as HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr, func_80016C64, func_80022C30, func_8007F694, func_800804EC, PSMTXConcat, PSVECAdd, HSD_JObjAnimAll, HSD_JObjRemoveAll, HSD_JObjSetupMatrixSub, and HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub.
Declared lbl_804D64FC through lbl_804D6550 as void* globals, with a comment that the range is initialized to zero in func_80067ABC and is probably pointers.
Added fpclassify support to include/math.h via FloatType, __fpclassifyf, __fpclassifyd, and fpclassify macro.
Updated callsites to use the new field names: xC became xC_playerID, x4_index became x4_fighterKind, xEC became xEC_groundVel, x21DC/x21E4 became callback fields, and x258 became x110_attr.x258.
smart_moves: Merged the header before fighter.c to reduce drift and unblock later fighter decomp work while keeping master synchronized.
Kept uncertain or not-yet-used attr/callback knowledge visible but caveated in comments instead of hiding everything behind anonymous filler.
Used offset-prefixed field names, preserving exact layout information while adding semantic names where evidence/datamining supported them.
Grouped the large character attribute table into a nested x110_attr struct, making callsites like walljump distinguish global Fighter fields from per-fighter attribute data.
Centralized needed extern/prototype declarations in headers, allowing removal of at least one local extern in player.c.
Updated existing callsites immediately after renaming fields, demonstrating that the layout was intended to remain source-compatible with current matched code.
decomp_lessons: For large structs that are still partly unknown, use xOFFSET_semanticName when a meaning is likely, and retain filler_xOFFSET ranges where evidence is missing.
When importing datamined struct knowledge ahead of full C sources, explicitly comment which members are confirmed or used and which are provisional.
Nested substructures can clarify known packed tables, but every callsite must be updated to the new access path to avoid accidentally treating an attr member as a top-level Fighter field.
Callback tables benefit from exact offset names plus semantic suffixes; this makes callsites like OnTakeDamage/OnDeath easier to audit while preserving the offset.
Central function prototypes reduce scattered local externs and make future decomp files easier to compile, but partial signatures should be marked with comments when uncertain.
Header PRs can be valuable independent milestones in decomp projects when a large implementation file is not yet ready.
naming_conventions: Fighter fields use xOFFSET_name, for example x4_fighterKind, xC_playerID, xEC_groundVel, x1998_shieldHealth, and x2064_ledgeCooldown.
Unknown padding uses filler_xSTART or filler_xSTART[END - START] style to preserve layout.
Nested attributes use x110_attr.xOFFSET_Name, for example x110_attr.x128_GroundFriction and x110_attr.x258.
Callback fields use xOFFSET_callback_Name, for example x21DC_callback_OnTakeDamage and x21E4_callback_OnDeath2.
Function prototypes remain mostly address-based func_80xxxxxx names where no stable semantic name exists, sometimes with comments naming suspected behavior.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added prototypes with comments pointing to asm source files, such as HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr from asm/melee/lb/lbcollision.s, func_80016C64 from lbarchive.s, PSMTXConcat from asm/dolphin/mtx/mtx.s, PSVECAdd from asm/dolphin/mtx/vec.s, and several HSD_JObj functions from sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.s.
Used partial signatures and void returns where exact signatures were not known, preserving compile utility without overstating certainty.
Added fpclassify inline helpers in math.h, likely to support code needing compiler/library-compatible floating-point classification.
Replaced local extern declarations with centralized header declarations, as shown by removal of func_80016C64 extern from player.c.
Maintained exact offsets in comments throughout structs, which is essential for matching decompiled code against existing assembly.

review_feedback.note: The provided PR context contains no issue comments, review comments, or reviews.
Author said not all Fighter::attr members and callbacks are used yet; unused/provisional cases were commented, and names were expected to be relatively accurate from datamining.

follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that merge fighter.c and validate or rename the provisional Fighter attr and callback fields.
Search for subsequent changes to x110_attr members to see which datamined names were confirmed or corrected.
Search for later replacement of func_80067ABC and other fighter.s address-based prototypes with semantic names.
Check if the fpclassify helpers in include/math.h were later moved, altered, or required for matching specific C files.
Search for PRs touching lbl_804D64FC..lbl_804D6550 to confirm whether these void* globals were all pointers.
